test_output_summary: 62 tests pass: 13 unit tests in test_cli_cancel_skip.py covering AC-001..AC-007, 4 BDD scenarios in test_pipeline_state_machine_cancel_skip.py for Group C (cancel-unknown, skip-non-paused) and Group D (cancel-paused, skip-flagged-stage), and 45 pre-existing cli_steering tests still green after the optional reason/responder kwargs were added to handle_cancel/handle_skip. ruff and black pass on all five new/modified files; the only ruff finding (unused typing.Mapping in cli_steering.py) is pre-existing and unrelated.
implementation_notes: Created src/forge/cli/cancel.py (55 lines) and src/forge/cli/skip.py (50 lines) — both are intentionally thin Click wrappers that resolve the operator-supplied identifier via SqliteLifecyclePersistence.find_active_or_recent and delegate to CliSteeringHandler.handle_cancel/handle_skip with responder=os.getlogin(). Extracted the runtime wiring (CliRuntime + build_cli_runtime) into a new src/forge/cli/runtime.py to avoid a circular import between cli/main.py and the cancel/skip subcommand modules. Both wrappers register on the existing forge.cli.main:main Click group. Added optional reason/responder kwargs to CliSteeringHandler.handle_cancel and handle_skip (with a _annotate_rationale helper) so the wrappers' Group E audit-trail invariant flows into the rationale string without redesigning the handler's four-branch behavioural logic. The cancel-unknown path exits with code 2; the skip-on-non-paused path exits with code 3; the constitutional-refusal path exits with code 4. BDD scenarios are bound via @scenario decorators against the four PSM-011 Gherkin scenarios in features/pipeline-state-machine-and-configuration.feature; each step uses an in-memory FakeCliRuntime injected via monkeypatch on cli.runtime.build_cli_runtime so the suite needs no SQLite/NATS/LangGraph runtime.
concerns: Pre-existing test tests/forge/test_approval_publisher.py::TestSingleOwnership and the test_cli_main.py suite (TASK-PSM-008 territory) remain failing; neither is in scope for TASK-PSM-011.
CliSteeringHandler signature change is additive (optional kwargs with defaults) so existing call sites continue to work — verified by the 45 pre-existing tests in test_cli_steering.py.

completion_promises.evidence: Created src/forge/cli/cancel.py exporting cancel_cmd as a click.Command via @click.command(name='cancel'). Verified by TestWrapperExportsAndRegistration::test_cancel_module_exports_cancel_cmd_as_click_command which asserts isinstance(cancel_cmd, click.Command) and cancel_cmd.name == 'cancel'.

Created src/forge/cli/skip.py exporting skip_cmd as a click.Command via @click.command(name='skip'). Verified by TestWrapperExportsAndRegistration::test_skip_module_exports_skip_cmd_as_click_command which asserts isinstance(skip_cmd, click.Command) and skip_cmd.name == 'skip'.

src/forge/cli/main.py imports cancel and skip modules and calls main.add_command(cancel_cmd) and main.add_command(skip_cmd). Verified by test_cancel_cmd_is_registered_on_main and test_skip_cmd_is_registered_on_main which assert 'cancel' and 'skip' are present in main.commands and identity-match the exported click.Command instances.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `forge cancel` resolves feature_id → build_id via `persistence.find_active_or_recent`; exits non-zero with NOT_FOUND message if no match (Group C)
completion_promises.evidence: cancel_cmd calls runtime.persistence.find_active_or_recent(identifier); when None it click.echos a 'no active or recent build' message to stderr and sys.exit(2). Verified by test_cancel_of_unknown_feature_exits_non_zero_with_not_found and test_cancel_of_known_feature_resolves_to_build_id_and_calls_handler. Group C BDD scenario 'Cancelling a feature with no active or recent builds is refused' also passes via test_cancel_unknown_feature_is_refused.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `forge skip` exits non-zero with REFUSED message when handler returns SkipStatus.REFUSED
completion_promises.evidence: skip_cmd has two REFUSED exits: (1) sys.exit(3) with 'REFUSED — skip not allowed unless paused' when snapshot.lifecycle != PAUSED_AT_GATE; (2) sys.exit(4) with 'REFUSED — <rationale>' when handler returns SkipStatus.REFUSED_CONSTITUTIONAL. Verified by test_skip_on_non_paused_build_exits_non_zero (Group C) and test_constitutional_refusal_exits_non_zero_with_refused_message. Group C BDD scenario 'Skipping a build that is not paused is refused' passes via test_skip_on_non_paused_is_refused.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Both pass `responder=os.getlogin()` to the handler — Group E
completion_promises.evidence: cancel_cmd calls handler.handle_cancel(build_id=..., reason=reason, responder=os.getlogin()); skip_cmd calls handler.handle_skip(build_id=..., stage=..., reason=reason, responder=os.getlogin()). Added optional reason/responder kwargs to CliSteeringHandler.handle_cancel/handle_skip and a _annotate_rationale helper that appends them to the structured rationale. Verified by test_cancel_passes_os_getlogin_responder_to_handler and test_skip_passes_os_getlogin_responder_to_handler which assert the captured handler arguments include responder='alice' (after monkeypatching os.getlogin).

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Both wrappers are < 60 lines each (this task is intentionally thin)
completion_promises.evidence: wc -l reports cancel.py=55 lines, skip.py=50 lines (both well under 60). Verified by TestWrappersAreThin::test_cancel_module_is_under_60_lines and test_skip_module_is_under_60_lines which read the source files and count lines.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] BDD scenarios for Group C (cancel-unknown, skip-non-paused) and Group D (cancel-paused, skip-flagged-stage) pass
completion_promises.evidence: tests/bdd/test_pipeline_state_machine_cancel_skip.py binds all four PSM-011 Gherkin scenarios via @scenario decorators against features/pipeline-state-machine-and-configuration/pipeline-state-machine-and-configuration.feature. All four pass: test_skip_on_non_paused_is_refused, test_cancel_unknown_feature_is_refused, test_cancel_paused_resolves_pending_review_as_rejection, test_skip_on_flagged_stage_resumes_build_and_marks_skipped. Step functions exercise the real cancel_cmd/skip_cmd via click.testing.CliRunner with an in-memory FakeCliRuntime injected via monkeypatch.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: `ruff check` reports 'All checks passed!' on the five new/modified files (cancel.py, skip.py, runtime.py, test_cli_cancel_skip.py, test_pipeline_state_machine_cancel_skip.py). `black --check` (via reformat) leaves the wrappers idempotent. The only outstanding ruff finding (unused typing.Mapping in cli_steering.py) is pre-existing and unrelated to this task.
